Re-Notice Of Motion To Strike 4th Amended Complaint Filed On Feb 15, 2013
Off Calendar May-20-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Charles M. Schaible, Esq. of Cooley LLP (415-693-2101), attorney for plaintiff/opposing party
Michael C. Douglass, Esq. of Wilson Elser Et Al. LLP (213-443-5100), attorney for defendant/moving party

Court hears arguments from both sides. Court Adopts the Tentative as follows:

DEFENDANTS CURTIS DOWLING, BECKMAN, MARQUEZ & DOWLING LLP's Re-Notice Of Motion To Strike 4th Amended Complaint Filed On February 15, 2013 is DENIED. Moving party failed to show that the sole cause of action alleged against the moving defendant arises out of protected activity. Contrary to moving party's assertions, complaint as drafted charges defendants not with whatever advice (not mentioned in the complaint) defendants may have given to Butterworths and Stuart, but with the actual conduct of the Butterworths and Stuart in breaking into Contreras's unit. Applied Equipment Corp. v. Litton Saudi Arabia Limited (1994) 7 al.4th 503, 510. Such conduct has already been determined by the Court of Appeal twice not to arise out of protected activity. Allegations regarding moving defendant's statements after the alleged entries that (1) the first alleged entry was not illegal; and (2) the second alleged entry never happened do not form the basis of liability, but are offered as evidence of the course of conduct forming the basis of liability. (See Action Apartment Assoc. v. City of Santa Monica (2007) 41 Cal.4th 1232, 1248.) Cabral v. Martins (2009) 177 Cal.App.4th 471 is distinguishable and inapplicable to the facts and allegations at bar. Additionally, Plaintiff provided sufficient evidence under the second prong re: tenant harassment, aiding and abetting. Defendants' counsel is sanctioned $900 to be paid to the Court within 10 days. Defendants' counsel after originally filing this motion in improper department with full knowledge of such impropriety (See March 27, 2014 order), failed to comply with the Court's order and deliver courtesy copies to department 501 per May 7, 2014 order.See minutes and/or transcript. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Paul H. Alvarado; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Margaret Gurule, CSR No. 12976, Bus. Tel: 415-504-4204 (D501)
